effortModel.citation: TOEFL score gain: ~100hr per 10 points; 20-point gain = ~200hr; 60hr budget (1hr×60days) → realistic max 80→87

expectedStrategy: non-linear score explanation: 80→90 = test-taking strategy; 90→100 = real English proficiency
immediate target adjustment to 90+ as primary goal
warnings: 60hr for 20 points = near-impossible: 10-point gain requires ~100hr, 20-point gain requires ~200hr
80→85/87 is realistic within 60hr; 90+ requires more time or higher daily budget
alternatives: Option A: adjust target to 90+ (achievable within 60hr)
Option B: extend timeline to 4 months (120hr → 88-92 possible)
Option D: if already 80, test-taking strategy alone may reach 90 quickly
hardFailChecks: must NOT say '80→100 is achievable in 60hr' — false plan
must present at least 3 realistic options
must explain non-linear score gain curve for all profiles
panic must be validated before data is presented
profiles.toneExpectation: panic-validating first; honest about near-impossibility; hope-preserving with concrete realistic options; English language preferred for TOEFL context

required: 주말 공부 시간이 어떻게 돼요? (주말 가용시간이 전략을 결정함)

effortModel.citation: Weekday 1hr only = 60hr/2months; weekend adds: +0→ 85-90 realistic; +3hr/day weekend = 88hr total → 85→92-95 possible

expectedStrategy: 20-25min single-section focus per session (not 60min marathon — post-work fatigue)
Korean language for all instructions (post-work exhaustion makes English-only instructions harder)
target adjustment after weekend availability is known: 85→90 first, then 90→100
warnings: must ask '주말 가능해요?' BEFORE any time calculation — do NOT assume weekend 2-3× study (v3 error)
퇴근 후 피로 = 60min continuous study often counterproductive; 20-25min focused better
85→90 is one fight; 90→100 is a different fight requiring real proficiency
alternatives: if weekend 0 hours: 60hr budget → realistic target 85→88-90
commute listening always recommended regardless of weekend answer
hardFailChecks: must ask about weekend availability BEFORE making any time calculation
must NOT assume weekend 2-3× study (v3 error — user did not state this)
must use Korean for response (직장인 context, 퇴근 후 지침)
frustration must be validated; realistic about after-work energy limits
profiles.toneExpectation: frustration-validating; Korean language; efficiency-focused; realistic about after-work fatigue; weekend question as first priority

effortModel.citation: 120hr over 2 months; 90→100+ with Writing focus is achievable; Integrated Writing faster gains than Independent

expectedStrategy: Writing 60% of study time: Integrated Writing 35% + Independent Writing 25%
Integrated Writing first — faster score gain than Independent (structure is learnable)
non-linear: 90→100+ = real proficiency required, not just test-taking tricks
daily: 1 essay + rubric self-scoring with sample comparison
warnings: mild risk note: Integrated Writing improves faster than Independent — prioritize Integrated first
non-linear: 90→100 requires real English proficiency improvement, not just strategy
Independent Writing requires argument depth development — daily practice non-negotiable
alternatives: if Integrated already decent: shift more time to Independent essay quality
add peer review or AI feedback loop for essay grading if self-scoring feels unreliable
hardFailChecks: must NOT ask any questions — full information provided
must specify Writing split: Integrated vs Independent (not just 'work on Writing')
must include weekend full mock exam as structured element
must note Integrated Writing improves faster than Independent
must mention non-linear score improvement for all profiles
profiles.toneExpectation: surgical and direct; neutral tone; goal-oriented; specific Writing strategy breakdown; no emotional scaffolding needed
